name: Reggie Workman
abstract: Reginald "Reggie" Workman (born June 26, 1937) is an American avant-garde jazz and hard bop double bassist, recognized for his work with both John Coltrane and Art Blakey, in addition to Alice Coltrane, Mal Waldron, Max Roach, Archie Shepp, Trio Three (with Oliver Lake and Andrew Cyrille), Trio Transition, the Reggie Workman Ensemble, an…

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Wild Magnolias, Freddie Hubbard Quintet, and McCoy Tyner Quintet

works.t: Carnival in Flanders: Here's That Rainy Day

about_quotes.speaker: Marilyn Crispell
about_quotes.evidence: She has collaborated with Anthony Braxton, Henry Grimes, and Reggie Workman
about_quotes.doc_title: Marilyn Crispell interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 21, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Cedar Walton
about_quotes.evidence: Me and uh, by this time it was Reggie Workman, we left to take a long-term engagement in uh The Five Spot... we had a trio there... So you took Workman out of the, out of the Blakey band? Yeah, me and him stopped at the same time
about_quotes.doc_title: Cedar Walton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Stewart
about_quotes.evidence: this particular band was Collective Black Artists Big Band. And it was a big band that was under the auspices of Reggie Workman... With Collective Black Artists there was Reggie Workman
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Stewart interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rome, New York, August 24, 2006

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie McLean
about_quotes.evidence: We had our time together in [Charles] Mingus’s band... Like Reggie Workman would call me and say, “Jackie, I’ve got a club that wants you to come down... And Reggie and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie McLean — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Rashied Ali and Henry Grimes
about_quotes.evidence: And Reggie Workman, who also was — I mean he had my real good friends in the band, you know?
about_quotes.doc_title: Rashied Ali and Henry Grimes interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, January 30, 2009

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: I jammed with him and Reggie Workman so we weren't like strangers.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
